openFindings.title: memory_update MCP tool bypasses Invariant 3: arbitrary tier promotion on any existing row
openFindings.evidence: handleMemoryUpdate validates importanceTier only against VALID_TIERS (line 67), passes through updateParams.importanceTier (line 87), calls vectorIndex.updateMemory which writes importance_tier = ? with no path check (vector-index-mutations.ts:399-403). MCP tool exposed via tool-schemas.ts:295. Defeats user-directed Invariant 3.
openFindings.securityFraming: attacker-controlled-content-injection: promoting a non-constitutional memory to tier=constitutional causes the auto-surface hook (hooks/memory-surface.ts:187) to return it in every search session's context prelude

openFindings.title: Cleanup script builds plan outside transaction (TOCTOU)
openFindings.evidence: const plan = buildPlan(database); const tx = database.transaction(() => applyCleanup(database, plan));
openFindings.iter5Verification: Confirmed real — cleanup opens its own SQLite connection (line 57); MCP server uses in-process withSpecFolderLock only; no cross-process coordination.

openFindings.title: FTS cleanup relies on assumed trigger with no runtime verification
Symlink bypass: path.resolve does not follow symlinks
openFindings.evidence: canonicalFilePath = path.resolve(parsed.filePath).replace(/\\/g, '/'); adjacent code-graph scan uses realpathSync explicitly with SECURITY comment. Design inconsistency within same codebase.
openFindings.securityFraming: privilege-escalation: symlink in specs/ pointing to /external/ or /z_future/ bypasses invariant
openFindings.iter5Verification: Confirmed — code-graph scan.ts:135 uses realpathSync with explicit SECURITY comment; memory-save + walker paths do not follow the same doctrine. Design-level inconsistency.

openFindings.title: Cleanup LIKE patterns miss rows with no leading slash
openFindings.evidence: file_path LIKE '%/z_future/%' - diverges from (^|/)z_future(/|$) regex semantics
openFindings.securityFraming: cleanup-evasion: attacker storing relative z_future/ path bypasses cleanup while matching runtime regex

openFindings.title: Cleanup --apply has no idempotence short-circuit
openFindings.evidence: no early-exit branch when before summary shows zero violations

openFindings.title: stripDeletedIdsFromMutationLedger is a silent no-op stub
Log-injection in save-time downgrade WARN (parser-controlled file_path logged verbatim)
openFindings.evidence: console.warn('[memory-save] ...', { file_path: parsed.filePath }) - no newline/control-char strip

openFindings.title: Silent tier downgrade still indexes attacker-declared-constitutional content; no governance audit row
openFindings.evidence: parsed.importanceTier = 'important'; save continues. No governance_audit insert on this branch.

openFindings.title: Walker DoS: unbounded readdirSync + per-directory whole .gitignore read
openFindings.evidence: loadGitignore uses readFileSync with no size cap; findFiles.walk has no depth / node-count bound.

openFindings.title: spec.md Status=In Progress contradicts verified delivered state
Stale _memory.continuity frontmatter in plan.md, decision-record.md, spec.md (completion_pct=20)
plan.md Implementation Phases + DoD checkboxes remain unchecked after completion
Parallel exclusion lists in spec-doc-paths.ts duplicate index-scope.ts invariants
SPEC_DOC_EXCLUDE_DIRS in memory-index-discovery.ts is a third exclusion source with partial overlap and 'iterations' granularity drift
No code back-references to packet 011, ADR-004, or ADR-005 anywhere in shipped implementation
openFindings.iter5Strengthening: Reinforced — memory-crud-update.ts is exactly the kind of file a maintainer working on packet 011 would not read because nothing signals that memory_update enforces (or is supposed to enforce) the same invariant.

openFindings.title: memory_update missing governance_audit record for tier changes
openFindings.evidence: memory_update writes mutation_ledger with generic reason 'memory_update: metadata update' but no governance_audit row for tier transitions to/from constitutional. Combined with P0-001, a successful attack leaves no typed audit signal.

openFindings.title: Symlink bypass escalation: design-level inconsistency within same codebase (code-graph uses realpathSync, memory-save does not)
openFindings.evidence: code-graph/handlers/scan.ts:135 explicit comment 'SECURITY: Canonicalize paths via realpathSync() to prevent symlink bypass'; memory-save.ts:306 uses path.resolve only. Design doctrine inconsistency.

openFindings.title: isConstitutionalPath recompiles regex per call
README.md rejection is defense-in-depth by coincidence
Two guard invocations with different input normalization
EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH blanket-excludes any 'dist' segment
Cleanup collectSummary.zFutureRows mixes OR conditions that delete cannot match
Cleanup console.error leaks absolute DB path on better-sqlite3 I/O failure
Cleanup produces no machine-readable report
Supply-chain: sqlite-vec native extension loaded unconditionally in cleanup
spec.md Section 13 open-question about README count (2 vs 3) is stale after ADR-005
feature_catalog and manual_testing_playbook carry no entry for the three invariants or index-scope helper
Decision-record does not document packet 010/011 composition for save-path defense-in-depth
Parent 026 graph-metadata.json child reference contains double-slash drift
Cleanup script diverges from sibling-script conventions (DB_PATH source, banners)
No test-visible access to compileSegmentPattern / normalizeIndexScopePath / isConstitutionalPath
index-scope.ts has no JSDoc on any exported function
Tests do not lock in that readme.md is excluded under /constitutional/
Operator-facing --verify has no CI hook documented
Function naming: shouldIndexForMemory ambiguous with RAM vs memory-system-index
update_memory silently invalidates constitutional cache on every tier change (no durable signal)
openFindings.evidence: clear_constitutional_cache() fires whenever importanceTier !== undefined; no durable record distinguishes legitimate tier changes from attempted promotions.

openFindings.title: mcp_server/README.md Invariants section omits memory_update UPDATE path
openFindings.evidence: README documents save-time guard and walker exclusions but does not enumerate every tier-write site; reader seeking 'all enforcement sites' would miss memory_update.

openFindings.title: No regression test for memory_update tier-promotion scenarios
openFindings.evidence: Existing suite covers only handleMemorySave + indexMemoryFileFromScan; no test asserts handleMemoryUpdate behavior when importanceTier='constitutional' is applied to a non-constitutional-path row. Compound with P0-001.

openFindings.title: checkpoint_restore revives arbitrary (file_path, importance_tier) pairs from snapshot with no invariant re-assertion
openFindings.evidence: restoreCheckpoint decompresses memory_snapshot blob (line 1492), parses JSON (line 1493), and writes rows via INSERT OR REPLACE (line 1544) or dynamic UPDATE (line 1552) using columns directly from snapshot including importance_tier and file_path. validateMemoryRow at line 1258 checks TYPES only (id is number, file_path is non-empty string, required fields present); does NOT re-assert isConstitutionalPath or shouldIndexForMemory. Attacker with tampered checkpoint (or prior P0-001-poisoned DB captured as snapshot) can re-hydrate arbitrary constitutional rows.
openFindings.securityFraming: attacker-controlled-content-injection via tampered-checkpoint restore; compound exploit chain with P0-001 (poison → checkpoint → bulk_delete audit → restore after retention sweep)

openFindings.title: applyPostInsertMetadata ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS allowlist includes importance_tier with no path check; every caller is structurally dependent on upstream guard
openFindings.evidence: ALLOWED_POST_INSERT_COLUMNS at line 53-62 includes 'importance_tier'. The dynamic SET builder at line 80-109 issues UPDATE memory_index SET importance_tier = ? with no inspection of the value against companion file_path. All current callers (create-record, lineage-state, reconsolidation, chunking-orchestrator, pe-gating, memory-save.ts:3050 governance, memory-save.ts:3235) pass parsed.importanceTier which has been downgraded by the save guard — but this is STRUCTURAL fragility. Any future caller bypasses Invariant 3 silently. Compound with P0-001, P0-002.

openFindings.title: memory_save direct-call bypasses walker's z_future/external exclusion (Invariant 1/2 enforced only on walker path)
openFindings.evidence: prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing at line 298 only runs the constitutional guard (line 310: if parsed.importanceTier === 'constitutional' && !isConstitutionalPath(canonicalFilePath) then downgrade). It does NOT call shouldIndexForMemory. A direct MCP call handleMemorySave({ filePath: '/abs/.opencode/specs/foo/z_future/bar.md' }) with any non-constitutional tier is not blocked. Walker exclusion only fires during memory_index_scan; direct saves bypass the walker. Invariant 1 (z_future NEVER indexed) and Invariant 2 (external NEVER indexed) hold for the walker but fail for direct-save path.
openFindings.securityFraming: Invariant-1/2 bypass via direct MCP call path
openFindings.refutationEvidence: Re-read of memory-save.ts:298-320 shows line 307 unconditionally calls shouldIndexForMemory(canonicalFilePath) and throws for z_future/external/z_archive paths BEFORE the constitutional tier check at line 310. Iter-6 reasoning misread line 307 as tier-gated; it is a standalone guard. Direct memory_save DOES enforce Invariants 1 and 2.

openFindings.title: P0-001 reachability re-check — confirmed exposed on public MCP surface with no admin/provenance gate
openFindings.evidence: memory_update is listed alongside memory_delete/memory_validate/memory_bulk_delete in L4:Mutation. Dispatch at memory-tools.ts:104 calls handleMemoryUpdate with no authz gate, admin-role check, or provenance requirement (unlike memory_save at tool-schemas.ts:223 which has provenanceSource/provenanceActor governance parameters). Any MCP client that can call memory_search can call memory_update with equivalent capability. P0-001 downgrade-trigger NOT met; severity stays P0.

resolvedFindings.reason: Re-read of memory-save.ts:307 shows shouldIndexForMemory is called unconditionally before the tier check at line 310; Invariants 1 and 2 ARE enforced on the direct-save path, not only on the walker path. Iter-6 evidence misread the guard scope.

convergenceVetoReason: Two P0s open (iter-5 P0-001 memory_update bypass + iter-6 P0-002 checkpoint_restore bypass); strategy P0 override rule blocks convergence regardless of iter-7 newFindingsRatio = 0.0

reinforcedFindings.note: Symlink-INTO-constitutional-pointing-OUT variant added: a symlink under /constitutional/ pointing at /z_future/ or /external/ causes a DOUBLE invariant violation (1 + 3) because memory-save.ts:306 uses path.resolve (no symlink follow) while canonical_file_path DB column is computed via getCanonicalPathKey (realpathSync). Result: file_path=symlink passes guard, canonical_file_path=real path violates Invariants. Wave 2 fix MUST use getCanonicalPathKey as guard input.
